item.ptxt: The structure of the master cylinder allows the piston to pop out, so observe the following:
The master cylinder requires careful handling. Do not allow the master cylinder to receive any impact, such as from being dropped. Do not reuse a master cylinder that has been dropped.
Do not scratch or damage the circumference of the piston. For the seal to function correctly, all sliding surfaces must not have any damage.
Do not pull out the master cylinder piston.
Make sure to release the vacuum from the brake booster (by removing a vacuum hose, etc.) before removing the master cylinder from the brake booster.
When installing the master cylinder, remove the protectors for the piston and the discharge port.
Apply the supplied grease to the outer diameter of the piston and the inner diameter of the brake booster.
Do not hold the master cylinder by the piston. (Hold the master cylinder by its body or its reservoir while transporting it.)
Do not pull out the master cylinder piston.
Do not strike or pinch the master cylinder piston, and do not cause any damage to the master cylinder piston by any other means.
Make sure to release the vacuum from the brake booster before removing the master cylinder from the brake booster.

When installing the master cylinder to the brake booster, or when removing the master cylinder from the brake booster, make sure that the master cylinder is kept horizontal or its tip faces downward (the piston faces upward) to prevent the master cylinder piston from falling off.

Do not allow any foreign objects to contaminate the master cylinder piston. If a foreign object gets on the piston, remove it by using a shop rag or a piece of cloth and then apply an even layer of lithium soap based glycol grease around the circumference (sliding part) of the piston
Do not use any other type of grease.
Stop the engine and depress the brake pedal several times until no vacuum is left in the brake booster.
If the brake fluid leaks onto any painted surface, wash off or remove the brake fluid completely.

s2.ptxt: Disconnect the mass air flow meter connector (*1).
Disconnect the purge VSV connector (*2).
Disconnect the 2 purge VSV vacuum hoses (*3).
Disconnect the purge line hose from the clamp (*4).
Disconnect the No. 2 ventilation hose from the air cleaner hose.
Lock the No. 1 air cleaner hose clamp, and then disconnect the No. 1 air cleaner hose from the throttle body.
Remove the 2 bolts and air cleaner cap.
Remove the air cleaner filter element from the air cleaner case.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE AIR CLEANER FILTER ELEMENT SUB-ASSEMBLY
REMOVE FRONT WIPER ARM AND BLADE ASSEMBLY LH

s2.ptxt: Remove the nut and the front wiper arm and blade assembly LH.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE FRONT WIPER ARM AND BLADE ASSEMBLY RH

s2.ptxt: Remove the nut and the front wiper arm and blade assembly RH.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE FRONT FENDER TO COWL SIDE SEAL LH

s2.ptxt: Disengage the claw and remove the front fender to cowl side seal LH.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE FRONT FENDER TO COWL SIDE SEAL RH

s2.ptxt: Disengage the claw and remove the front fender to cowl side seal RH.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE COWL TOP VENTILATOR LOUVER SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Remove the 2 clips.
Disengage the 4 claws and remove the cowl top ventilator louver sub-assembly.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE WINDSHIELD WIPER MOTOR AND LINK ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Disconnect the connector.
Remove the 4 bolts and the windshield wiper motor and link assembly.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE COWL TOP OUTER FRONT PANEL SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Disengage the clamp.
Remove the 4 nuts, the 4 bolts and the cowl top outer front panel sub-assembly.

s-1.ptxt: DISCONNECT CLUTCH RESERVOIR TUBE (for Manual Transaxle)

s2.ptxt: Slide the clip and disconnect the clutch reservoir tube from the brake master cylinder sub-assembly.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE BRAKE MASTER CYLINDER SUB-ASSEMBLY (for TMC Made)

s2.ptxt: Disconnect the connector.
Using SST, disconnect the 6 brake lines and remove the brake master cylinder to way tube.
Remove the 2 nuts, the front brake tube way, the O-ring and the brake master cylinder sub-assembly.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE BRAKE MASTER CYLINDER SUB-ASSEMBLY (for TMMK Made without VSC)

s2.ptxt: Disconnect the connector.
Using SST, disconnect the 6 brake lines and remove the brake master cylinder to way tube.
Remove the 2 nuts, the front brake tube way, the O-ring and the brake master cylinder sub-assembly.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE BRAKE MASTER CYLINDER SUB-ASSEMBLY (for TMMK Made with VSC)

s2.ptxt: Disconnect the connector.
Using SST, disconnect the 6 brake lines and remove the brake master cylinder to way tube.
Remove the 2 nuts, the front brake tube way, the O-ring and the brake master cylinder sub-assembly.
